title: 2026 Oscar Predictions: Can Sinners Become the New Frontrunner?
text: This Awards Daily article examines whether the vampire horror film Sinners, directed by Ryan Coogler, can win Best Picture after receiving a record-breaking 16 Oscar nominations. The unprecedented nomination count represents both an advantage and liability. Historically, films receiving the most nominations don't always triumph—only Gone with the Wind and Oppenheimer won Best Picture among preferential-ballot era films with 13+ nominations. The analyst emphasizes Sinners' commercial success and broad appeal across multiple craft categories. She contrasts it with One Battle After Another, which led with 13 nominations but lacks visual effects and costume design recognition. Stone makes a forceful case that the film's cultural impact and box office performance demonstrate genuine achievement rather than demographic considerations. She notes that no Black director has won Best Picture in 98 years of Academy history, though she frames Coogler's potential victory as earned rather than symbolic. Despite expert consensus favoring One Battle After Another, Stone predicts Sinners will claim Best Picture, with Coogler winning Best Director—making it historically significant as only the third instance of a Black director's film winning Picture without Director honors.
